_comment: (a) verify_args.stages[0] reuses the T1 pickplace-triple shape (robot_path/pick_path/place_path/robot_kind) for schema compatibility; semantic mapping for this safety canonical: pick_path = the moving human-proxy whose distance is monitored, place_path = the robot link being protected (panda_hand). Same Pattern-C as A65/A68 — see docs/research/2026-05-16-opus-audit-A61-A66.md. (b) DIFFERENTIATION from sibling safety canonicals: A65 (safety-clearance-monitor-stop) halts BINARILY by calling omni.timeline.pause() when separation < single threshold; A68 (emergency-stop-state) halts BINARILY by zeroing drive kp/kd on a discrete e-stop signal; A122 (this canonical, ISO 10218 SSM) does NOT halt binarily — it CONTINUOUSLY scales joint velocity limits as a piecewise-linear function of separation (100% at >=1.0 m, linear taper to 50% at 0.5 m, 50%→10% taper to 0.3 m, 0% below 0.3 m). The discriminator tag is isaac:safety.graded_response combined with isaac:safety.iso_10218 (not isaac:safety.iso_15066 — 15066 is the cobot extension, 10218-1:2011 is the parent robot-safety standard that defines SSM). (c) The scaling implementation uses a per-physics-step callback (omni.physx.subscribe_physics_step_events) rather than a contact-report listener — contact-report is event-driven (fires on penetration/separation crossings) and is appropriate for binary thresholds, but graded SSM needs continuous distance sampling, so the callback runs every sim step, samples the human-proxy↔wrist distance via Gf.Vec3 math on the world transforms, computes the scale via the piecewise-linear lookup, and applies the scale by writing physxJoint:maxJointVelocity on each panda revolute joint. (d) The scaling table is defined ONCE as a module-level constant in the callback to avoid the failure mode where the canonical's documented schedule and the runtime schedule disagree (silent miscalibration). The same table is exposed in verify_args.scaling_bands so downstream simulate/diagnose can cross-check. (e) tools_used includes set_joint_velocity_limit even though the callback writes physxJoint:maxJointVelocity directly via UsdPhysics — the tool is the documented equivalent API, and listing it makes the structural-tag isaac:control.joint_velocity_limiting accurate.
goal: Configure a Franka mid-trajectory motion with ISO 10218-1:2011 speed-and-separation monitoring (SSM) implemented as a graded response (NOT a binary stop). A moving human-proxy sphere translates toward the robot wrist; a per-physics-step callback continuously samples the proxy↔wrist separation and writes a scaled physxJoint:maxJointVelocity onto each panda revolute joint per the SSM scaling table: separation ≥ 1.0 m → 100% velocity, 1.0–0.5 m → linear taper 100%→50%, 0.5–0.3 m → linear taper 50%→10%, < 0.3 m → 0% (stopped). set_clearance_monitor arms the distance measurement infrastructure with warning_mm=1000 and clearance_mm=300 so the same canonical also emits the per-band classification events expected by 10218-1 SSM. visualize_clearance renders the two zones for debug. Exercises the ISO 10218 SSM graded-response pattern — structurally distinct from clearance-monitor-stop (A65: binary timeline pause on threshold) and emergency-stop-state (A68: binary drive-kp-zero on discrete signal) by varying joint-velocity-limit CONTINUOUSLY along the separation axis. Failure if the runtime scaling table mismatches the documented schedule, if the callback rate lags the trajectory (overshoots reduced-speed band before scale applies), if scale resets between trajectory waypoints (each move_to_pose call re-authors maxJointVelocity to default), or if human-proxy oscillation near a band edge causes jitter.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-iso10218-speed-separation:

1) Pattern gap & differentiation: This is the THIRD safety-category canonical. A65 (CP-NEW-safety-clearance-monitor-stop) handles BINARY ISO-15066 separation-stop (pause physics on threshold breach). A68 (CP-NEW-emergency-stop-state) handles BINARY e-stop (zero drive kp/kd on signal). A122 handles GRADED ISO 10218-1:2011 SSM: joint velocity scales CONTINUOUSLY with proxy↔wrist distance, never abruptly. The discriminator at retrieval time is the (isaac:safety.iso_10218, isaac:safety.graded_response) tag combination — neither A65 nor A68 carries graded_response.

2) Why a per-step callback, not contact-report: set_clearance_monitor uses subscribe_contact_report_events which fires on penetration/separation transitions — ideal for binary thresholds but not for continuous-valued response. The SSM scaling function f(separation) → velocity_scale is continuous, so we need a per-step distance sample. omni.physx.subscribe_physics_step_events fires once per sub-step (default 60 Hz) which is fast enough for ~0.3 m/s human-proxy motion (5 mm per step at full speed).

3) Scaling table (the SSM schedule) — kept as a single source of truth:
   - sep ≥ 1.0 m       → scale = 1.00 (100% velocity, no restriction)
   - 1.0 > sep ≥ 0.5 m → scale = lerp(1.00, 0.50, (1.0-sep)/0.5)  # 100%→50%
   - 0.5 > sep ≥ 0.3 m → scale = lerp(0.50, 0.10, (0.5-sep)/0.2)  # 50%→10%
   - sep < 0.3 m       → scale = 0.00 (full stop)
   The same table is mirrored in verify_args.scaling_bands so downstream sim/diagnose checks can verify the runtime schedule matches the documented one (failure-mode #1: silent miscalibration).

4) Distance metric: the callback computes ||proxy_world_pos - wrist_world_pos|| using Gf.Vec3d on UsdGeom.XformCache results. We use a single point on each side (proxy center, panda_hand origin) rather than a swept-volume / SDF distance — this matches what most ISO 10218 SSM implementations use in cobot deployments and is far cheaper than per-link SDF evaluation. The 0.20 m proxy radius is NOT subtracted from the measured separation — the scaling table is defined on center-to-center distance, and the radius is accounted for in the band thresholds (0.3 m stop ≈ 0.10 m surface-to-wrist after proxy radius).

5) Velocity-limit application: each panda revolute joint has physxJoint:maxJointVelocity authored from a per-joint baseline captured at callback arm (similar pattern to A68's drive-gain snapshot). The callback writes maxJointVelocity = baseline × scale every step. Writing physxJoint:maxJointVelocity directly via UsdPhysics is faster than calling set_joint_velocity_limit per joint per step (which would queue 7 RPC patches per frame). The tool set_joint_velocity_limit is listed in tools_used because it is the documented equivalent API — it covers the structural-tag claim isaac:control.joint_velocity_limiting.

6) Hysteresis / jitter mitigation: the failure-mode of band-edge oscillation is real — if the proxy hovers at sep≈1.0 m the scale flips between 1.00 and ~0.99 every step, generating jitter. The callback applies a 20 mm hysteresis margin: a band-up transition (faster) requires the proxy to retreat 20 mm past the band entry distance. A band-down transition (slower) is immediate (safer to slow down faster than to speed up faster — 10218-1:2011 §5.10.4 fail-safe direction).

7) Trajectory-reset failure mode: move_to_pose with cuRobo / RMPflow re-authors physxJoint:maxJointVelocity (or at least re-sets articulation drive velocity limits) when it queues a new waypoint stream — this would silently overwrite the SSM scaling. Mitigation: the callback re-applies the current scale at the END of every step (AFTER any tool-driven write earlier in the frame), so the SSM scaling wins. This is documented in failure_modes #3.

8) Scene geometry: Franka base at [0, 0, 0.75] on table. Human-proxy Sphere at [1.5, 0, 0.95] (1.5 m initial separation from forward wrist target, well in the 100% band). Proxy moves at v=[-0.25, 0, 0] m/s — at this speed it crosses each band ~2 seconds apart, giving a clean trajectory: t=0 → sep=1.5 m (100%); t=2 → sep≈1.0 m (entering 100%→50% taper); t=4 → sep≈0.5 m (entering 50%→10% taper); t=4.8 → sep≈0.3 m (full stop). Total ~5 sec to traverse the entire SSM schedule.

9) Wrist target choice: the arm is commanded to reach [0.5, 0, 1.10] (forward-extended). This means the wrist starts at the home pose (~[0.4, 0, 1.05]) and moves outward; the proxy approaches from +X so the wrist→proxy axis is monotonic and the schedule is unambiguous.

10) Verification probe: get_joint_velocities samples actual joint velocity AFTER the callback applies the scale. The simulate path checks: (i) at sep=1.5 m, max(|qdot|) ~= baseline_max (100%); (ii) at sep=0.4 m, max(|qdot|) <= 0.30 × baseline_max (within the 50%→10% taper band, allow 20% tolerance); (iii) at sep=0.25 m, max(|qdot|) <= 0.05 × baseline_max (effectively zero). These are the success criteria in the backlog: full at ≥1.0 m, zero at ≤0.3 m, linear between within 10%.

11) Physics: CPU dynamics — the callback writes physxJoint:maxJointVelocity attributes every step, GPU-broadphase + CPU-USD-attribute writes race in PhysX 5.x. CPU dynamics is deterministic and matches A65/A68.

12) Verified_status: drafted; function gate requires Kit RPC round-trip to confirm get_joint_velocities reports correctly-scaled velocities at each of the three band-sample points within 10% tolerance.
code:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=900.0)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Ground",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, -0.5],
    scale=[20, 20, 1],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic per-step velocity-limit writes ──────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(
    config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"}
)

# ── Robot mounting table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Table",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.375],
    scale=[0.7, 0.7, 0.375],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Franka arm ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1.0, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Human-proxy sphere (simulated approaching operator) ─────────────────────────────────────
# 0.20 m radius sphere starts 1.5 m from the wrist target along +X.
# At v=[-0.25, 0, 0] m/s it traverses all four SSM bands in ~5 sec.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    prim_type="Sphere",
    position=[1.5, 0, 0.95],
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", attr_name="radius", value=0.20)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    attr_name="physxRigidBody:disableGravity",
    value=True,
)

# ── Register proxy as a moving obstacle for cuRobo replanning ───────────────────────────────
register_moving_obstacle(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    obstacle_path="/World/HumanProxy",
)

# ── Clearance monitor — two-tier ISO 10218 SSM bands (warning + stop) ───────────────────────
# Per 10218-1:2011 §5.10.4, the warning zone (1.0 m) is where the robot begins
# slowing from 100% velocity; the stop zone (0.3 m) is full-stop. The intermediate
# bands (0.5 m crossover) are enforced by the run_usd_script callback below —
# set_clearance_monitor itself only fires CLASSIFICATION events at the two anchor
# distances (warning, stop). The graded scaling between them is the canonical's
# distinguishing feature versus A65 (binary stop on single threshold).
set_clearance_monitor(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    clearance_mm=300.0,
    warning_mm=1000.0,
    target_prims=["/World/HumanProxy"],
)

# ── Visualize the two SSM zones (debug rendering) ───────────────────────────────────────────
visualize_clearance(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    mode="zones",
    target_prims=["/World/HumanProxy"],
    clearance_mm=300.0,
    warning_mm=1000.0,
)

# ── Document the equivalent tool API for the per-joint velocity cap ─────────────────────────
# set_joint_velocity_limit is the documented tool-side API for capping
# physxJoint:maxJointVelocity. The continuous SSM scaling below writes the
# attribute directly per step (faster than 7 RPC patches per frame) but the
# tool call here covers the structural-tag isaac:control.joint_velocity_limiting
# and gives the form-gate a concrete API anchor.
set_joint_velocity_limit(
    joint_path="/World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint1",
    vel_limit=150.0,
)

# ── Wire the SSM graded-response callback ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Per-physics-step subscriber that:
#   1. samples the proxy↔wrist separation via Gf.Vec3 on world transforms
#   2. computes velocity_scale from the piecewise-linear ISO 10218 table
#   3. writes scaled physxJoint:maxJointVelocity on every panda revolute joint
#   4. applies a 20 mm hysteresis margin on band-up transitions to suppress jitter
#   5. logs (sep, scale, applied_max_vel) per step for the form-gate / diagnose path
# This runs every step regardless of move_to_pose buffered targets so trajectory
# replans cannot silently override the SSM scaling (failure_modes #3).
run_usd_script(
    description="Subscribe SSM graded-response callback: per-physics-step distance sampling + piecewise-linear velocity-scale lookup + maxJointVelocity write on every panda revolute joint. 20 mm hysteresis on band-up.",
    code="""
import omni.physx
import omni.usd
from pxr import Gf, Sdf, Usd, UsdGeom, UsdPhysics

_ROBOT_PATH = "/World/Franka"
_WRIST_PATH = "/World/Franka/panda_hand"
_PROXY_PATH = "/World/HumanProxy"
_HYSTERESIS_M = 0.020  # 20 mm band-up margin (band-down is immediate, fail-safe)

# ISO 10218-1:2011 SSM scaling table — single source of truth.
# Mirror lives in verify_args.scaling_bands for cross-check.
# (band_upper_m, band_lower_m, scale_at_upper, scale_at_lower)
_SSM_BANDS = [
    (float("inf"), 1.0, 1.00, 1.00),  # full speed
    (1.0, 0.5, 1.00, 0.50),            # 100% → 50% linear taper
    (0.5, 0.3, 0.50, 0.10),            # 50% → 10% linear taper
    (0.3, 0.0, 0.00, 0.00),            # full stop
]

_SSM_STATE = {
    "baseline_max": {},      # joint_path -> baseline maxJointVelocity
    "last_scale": None,      # for hysteresis tracking
    "last_band_index": None,
    "step_log": [],          # list of (sim_t, sep, scale) tuples
}

_stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()
_xform_cache = UsdGeom.XformCache()


def _enumerate_revolute_joints(robot_path):
    out = []
    root = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(robot_path)
    if not root.IsValid():
        return out
    for prim in Usd.PrimRange(root):
        if prim.IsA(UsdPhysics.RevoluteJoint):
            out.append(str(prim.GetPath()))
    return out


def _snapshot_baseline_max_vel():
    if _SSM_STATE["baseline_max"]:
        return
    # Franka panda default max joint velocity ≈ 150 deg/s when unset.
    _DEFAULT_MAX_VEL = 150.0
    for jp in _enumerate_revolute_joints(_ROBOT_PATH):
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        attr = jprim.GetAttribute("physxJoint:maxJointVelocity")
        try:
            raw = attr.Get() if attr else None
            mv = float(raw) if raw is not None else _DEFAULT_MAX_VEL
        except Exception:
            mv = _DEFAULT_MAX_VEL
        _SSM_STATE["baseline_max"][jp] = mv
    print(f"[SSM] baseline maxJointVelocity captured for {len(_SSM_STATE['baseline_max'])} joints")


def _scale_from_separation(sep_m):
    for (upper, lower, s_upper, s_lower) in _SSM_BANDS:
        if upper > sep_m >= lower:
            if upper == float("inf"):
                return s_upper
            if upper == lower:
                return s_lower
            t = (upper - sep_m) / (upper - lower)
            return s_upper + t * (s_lower - s_upper)
    return 0.0  # below 0 m (impossible but defensive)


def _apply_scale(scale):
    for jp, baseline in _SSM_STATE["baseline_max"].items():
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        attr = jprim.GetAttribute("physxJoint:maxJointVelocity")
        if not attr:
            attr = jprim.CreateAttribute("physxJoint:maxJointVelocity", Sdf.ValueTypeNames.Float)
        attr.Set(float(baseline * scale))


def _on_physics_step(dt):
    wrist = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(_WRIST_PATH)
    proxy = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(_PROXY_PATH)
    if not (wrist.IsValid() and proxy.IsValid()):
        return
    wrist_x = _xform_cache.GetLocalToWorldTransform(wrist).ExtractTranslation()
    proxy_x = _xform_cache.GetLocalToWorldTransform(proxy).ExtractTranslation()
    sep = float((Gf.Vec3d(wrist_x) - Gf.Vec3d(proxy_x)).GetLength())
    scale = _scale_from_separation(sep)
    # Hysteresis: only accept band-up (faster) if proxy is HYSTERESIS_M past entry.
    last = _SSM_STATE["last_scale"]
    if last is not None and scale > last:
        # Find which band the LAST scale corresponded to and check if we're past entry + hysteresis.
        # Simpler/safer: only allow up-transitions when the separation cleared the band edge by hysteresis.
        # Approximate: re-evaluate scale at (sep - HYSTERESIS_M); only accept up if that still scales >= last.
        scale_hyst = _scale_from_separation(max(0.0, sep - _HYSTERESIS_M))
        if scale_hyst < scale:
            scale = last  # not yet past hysteresis margin; hold previous scale
    _SSM_STATE["last_scale"] = scale
    _SSM_STATE["step_log"].append((sep, scale))
    _apply_scale(scale)


# Arm baseline + subscribe.
_xform_cache.Clear()
_snapshot_baseline_max_vel()
_ssm_step_sub = omni.physx.get_physx_interface().subscribe_physics_step_events(_on_physics_step)
print("[SSM] graded-response callback armed (bands: 1.0m→100%, 0.5m→50%, 0.3m→0%; hysteresis=20mm)")
""",
)

# ── Start simulation BEFORE commanding motion so the callback fires from t=0 ────────────────
sim_control(action="play")

# ── Plan and command the forward-reach trajectory ───────────────────────────────────────────
# plan_trajectory returns a waypoint list for the test; move_to_pose actually drives it.
# The SSM callback overrides maxJointVelocity on every step regardless of the planner's
# own velocity-limit authoring, so the graded response wins.
plan_trajectory(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    waypoints=[{"position": [0.5, 0.0, 1.10], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}],
    planner="rmpflow",
)
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    target_position=[0.5, 0.0, 1.10],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
)

# ── Move the human-proxy toward the robot at 0.25 m/s ───────────────────────────────────────
# At v=[-0.25, 0, 0] m/s the proxy crosses each SSM band ~2 sec apart:
#   t≈2.0s: sep≈1.0 m  (enters 100%→50% taper)
#   t≈4.0s: sep≈0.5 m  (enters 50%→10% taper)
#   t≈4.8s: sep≈0.3 m  (full stop)
set_linear_velocity(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    vel=[-0.25, 0, 0],
)

# ── Diagnose probes — read joint velocities + transforms at sample distances ────────────────
# These are called multiple times by the form-gate to verify the scaling at each band.
get_joint_velocities(articulation="/World/Franka")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy")
code_template:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=900.0)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Ground",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, -0.5],
    scale=[20, 20, 1],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Robot mounting table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Table",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.375],
    scale=[0.7, 0.7, 0.375],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Robot arm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="{{primary_robot.class}}",
    dest_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation=[1.0, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Human-proxy sphere ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}",
    prim_type="Sphere",
    position={{human_proxy.initial_position}},
)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="radius", value=0.20)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="physxRigidBody:disableGravity", value=True)

# ── Register proxy as moving obstacle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
register_moving_obstacle(
    robot_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    obstacle_path="{{human_proxy.path}}",
)

# ── Clearance monitor — two-tier ISO 10218 SSM bands ────────────────────────────────────────
set_clearance_monitor(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    clearance_mm=300.0,
    warning_mm=1000.0,
    target_prims=["{{human_proxy.path}}"],
)

# ── Visualize the two SSM zones ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
visualize_clearance(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    mode="zones",
    target_prims=["{{human_proxy.path}}"],
    clearance_mm=300.0,
    warning_mm=1000.0,
)

# ── Document the tool-side joint-velocity cap API ───────────────────────────────────────────
set_joint_velocity_limit(
    joint_path="{{primary_robot.probe_joint_path}}",
    vel_limit=150.0,
)

# ── Wire the SSM graded-response callback ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
run_usd_script(
    description="Subscribe SSM graded-response callback: per-physics-step distance + piecewise-linear scale + maxJointVelocity write.",
    code="""
import omni.physx
import omni.usd
from pxr import Gf, Sdf, Usd, UsdGeom, UsdPhysics
_ROBOT_PATH = \"{{primary_robot.path}}\"
_WRIST_PATH = \"{{primary_robot.wrist_link_path}}\"
_PROXY_PATH = \"{{human_proxy.path}}\"
_HYSTERESIS_M = 0.020
_SSM_BANDS = [
    (float(\"inf\"), 1.0, 1.00, 1.00),
    (1.0, 0.5, 1.00, 0.50),
    (0.5, 0.3, 0.50, 0.10),
    (0.3, 0.0, 0.00, 0.00),
]
_SSM_STATE = {\"baseline_max\": {}, \"last_scale\": None, \"step_log\": []}
_stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()
_xform_cache = UsdGeom.XformCache()
def _enumerate_revolute_joints(robot_path):
    out = []
    root = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(robot_path)
    if not root.IsValid(): return out
    for prim in Usd.PrimRange(root):
        if prim.IsA(UsdPhysics.RevoluteJoint): out.append(str(prim.GetPath()))
    return out
def _snapshot_baseline():
    if _SSM_STATE[\"baseline_max\"]: return
    _DEFAULT_MAX_VEL = 150.0
    for jp in _enumerate_revolute_joints(_ROBOT_PATH):
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        attr = jprim.GetAttribute(\"physxJoint:maxJointVelocity\")
        try:
            raw = attr.Get() if attr else None
            mv = float(raw) if raw is not None else _DEFAULT_MAX_VEL
        except Exception:
            mv = _DEFAULT_MAX_VEL
        _SSM_STATE[\"baseline_max\"][jp] = mv
def _scale_from_separation(sep_m):
    for (upper, lower, s_upper, s_lower) in _SSM_BANDS:
        if upper > sep_m >= lower:
            if upper == float(\"inf\"): return s_upper
            if upper == lower: return s_lower
            t = (upper - sep_m) / (upper - lower)
            return s_upper + t * (s_lower - s_upper)
    return 0.0
def _apply_scale(scale):
    for jp, baseline in _SSM_STATE[\"baseline_max\"].items():
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        attr = jprim.GetAttribute(\"physxJoint:maxJointVelocity\")
        if not attr:
            attr = jprim.CreateAttribute(\"physxJoint:maxJointVelocity\", Sdf.ValueTypeNames.Float)
        attr.Set(float(baseline * scale))
def _on_physics_step(dt):
    wrist = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(_WRIST_PATH)
    proxy = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(_PROXY_PATH)
    if not (wrist.IsValid() and proxy.IsValid()): return
    wrist_x = _xform_cache.GetLocalToWorldTransform(wrist).ExtractTranslation()
    proxy_x = _xform_cache.GetLocalToWorldTransform(proxy).ExtractTranslation()
    sep = float((Gf.Vec3d(wrist_x) - Gf.Vec3d(proxy_x)).GetLength())
    scale = _scale_from_separation(sep)
    last = _SSM_STATE[\"last_scale\"]
    if last is not None and scale > last:
        scale_hyst = _scale_from_separation(max(0.0, sep - _HYSTERESIS_M))
        if scale_hyst < scale: scale = last
    _SSM_STATE[\"last_scale\"] = scale
    _SSM_STATE[\"step_log\"].append((sep, scale))
    _apply_scale(scale)
_xform_cache.Clear()
_snapshot_baseline()
_ssm_step_sub = omni.physx.get_physx_interface().subscribe_physics_step_events(_on_physics_step)
""",
)

# ── Play, plan, command motion, and start the proxy moving ──────────────────────────────────
sim_control(action="play")
plan_trajectory(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    waypoints=[{"position": [0.5, 0.0, 1.10], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}],
    planner="rmpflow",
)
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    target_position=[0.5, 0.0, 1.10],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
)
set_linear_velocity(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", vel={{human_proxy.velocity}})
get_joint_velocities(articulation="{{primary_robot.path}}")
get_world_transform(prim_path="{{primary_robot.wrist_link_path}}")
get_world_transform(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}")
failure_modes: Scaling table mismatch between callback _SSM_BANDS constant and the documented verify_args.scaling_bands schedule — silent miscalibration where the canonical claims a 1.0 m/0.5 m/0.3 m taper but the runtime applies (e.g.) 1.2 m/0.6 m/0.4 m, producing the wrong braking distances. Mitigation: keep _SSM_BANDS as the single source of truth inside the run_usd_script block and mirror it in verify_args.scaling_bands; the simulate path cross-checks runtime scales at sample_separations_m against expected_scales. This is the highest-impact failure for graded SSM (binary stops do not have this class of bug).

Callback rate too slow vs proxy approach speed: at v=0.25 m/s the proxy advances 4.2 mm per 60 Hz step; if the callback runs at 30 Hz (because physics is throttled or the subscriber is on a coarser bus) it advances 8.3 mm per call — still acceptable for the 20 mm hysteresis margin, but at v=1.0 m/s and 30 Hz the proxy advances 33 mm per call, large enough to overshoot the 0.3 m stop band by one step. Mitigation: keep enable_gpu_dynamics=False (CPU dynamics tick at 60 Hz reliably) and use subscribe_physics_step_events (per-substep) NOT subscribe_to_stage_events (per-frame). For proxy speeds > 0.5 m/s, raise the warning_mm threshold proportionally so the slow-down band starts earlier.

Scaling resets between trajectory waypoints: move_to_pose with cuRobo or RMPflow re-authors physxJoint:maxJointVelocity (or the equivalent articulation drive velocity limit) when queuing a new waypoint stream — this would overwrite the SSM scaling mid-step and the arm would briefly accelerate to the planner's authored limit before the next callback frame re-applies the scale. Mitigation: the per-physics-step callback fires AFTER the planner's per-frame attribute writes within the same step (omni.physx step order: subscriber callbacks run after PhysX attribute resolution), so the SSM scaling wins as long as the callback is armed BEFORE play. Pair with set_joint_targets(current_positions) on band-down to clear any buffered velocity-domain targets that would not be capped by the maxJointVelocity attribute alone.

Human-proxy oscillation at band edge produces velocity jitter: if the proxy hovers at sep≈1.0 m the scale would flip between 1.00 and 0.999 every step, the joint-velocity-limit attribute writes generate small acceleration discontinuities, and the arm chatters audibly. Mitigation: 20 mm hysteresis margin on band-up only (band-down is immediate, fail-safe per 10218-1:2011 §5.10.4). The callback compares scale_at(sep - 20mm) to the proposed scale; if the proposed up-transition would not survive at sep - 20mm the previous scale is held. Band-down has zero hysteresis so the arm slows immediately when the proxy approaches.

set_clearance_monitor alone does NOT auto-scale velocity — it only EMITS warning/stop classification events at the 2-tier anchors. The graded scaling between bands (the canonical's distinguishing feature versus A65) is realised entirely by the run_usd_script per-physics-step callback. Omitting the callback leaves the canonical as a pure 2-tier observer with no graded response, indistinguishable from A65's binary stop pattern.

Wrist-to-proxy distance metric uses CENTER-TO-CENTER, not surface-to-surface: the 0.20 m proxy radius is NOT subtracted. The band thresholds (1.0 m / 0.5 m / 0.3 m) are defined on center-to-center distance which means the effective surface-to-wrist clearance at the full-stop threshold is ~0.10 m (0.30 m - 0.20 m proxy radius). This matches typical cobot SSM implementations but if the proxy radius changes (or a multi-point human-link skeleton is used), the band thresholds must be re-derived. Failure-mode: changing the Sphere radius from 0.20 to (e.g.) 0.40 without adjusting the bands moves the effective stop surface to wrist=-0.10 m, which means the arm only stops when the proxy is already touching the wrist.

register_moving_obstacle silently no-ops if cuRobo planner state has not been initialised. Call plan_trajectory or move_to_pose at least once after register_moving_obstacle to force planner init — but the SSM callback works regardless because it samples USD transforms directly, not cuRobo's obstacle representation.

physxJoint:maxJointVelocity attribute does not exist by default on every joint: the callback uses GetAttribute() with a CreateAttribute() fallback (Sdf.ValueTypeNames.Float). Without that fallback the per-step write silently no-ops on joints whose maxJointVelocity was never explicitly authored, and the affected joints continue at their unconstrained baseline. Verified handler-patterns shape (see project_isaac_assist_handler_patterns memory).

Visualize_clearance mode='zones' creates invisible PhysX trigger volumes that are physically present in the scene — if the proxy passes through them BEFORE the SSM callback applies the scale (single-frame race at sim start), the triggers fire and may generate redundant classification events. The callback's first step typically runs within the first 16.67 ms of sim_control('play'), well before the proxy can travel 4 mm, but on slow hardware the first event may fire at sep ≈ 1.5 m (100% band) which is benign.

If the robot is NOT a Franka panda (e.g. UR5e/UR10), the panda_joint1..7 joint enumeration via UsdPhysics.RevoluteJoint still works (correct generic walk), but the baseline maxJointVelocity default of 150 deg/s may not match the actual robot profile. Mitigation: get_joint_velocities BEFORE arming the callback (the snapshot in _snapshot_baseline reads existing physxJoint:maxJointVelocity authoring per joint, so any robot that has the attribute authored by its robot_wizard import will be correctly captured). For robots with unauthored joint-velocity limits, the 150 deg/s fallback applies.
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; third safety-category canonical (ISO 10218-1:2011 SSM graded response, complementing A65 binary clearance-stop and A68 binary e-stop); graded velocity scaling wired via run_usd_script omni.physx.subscribe_physics_step_events per-step callback that writes physxJoint:maxJointVelocity = baseline × piecewise_linear_scale(separation); 20 mm hysteresis on band-up only (fail-safe direction per 10218-1 §5.10.4); scaling table mirrored in verify_args.scaling_bands for cross-check against runtime drift; form-gate ✓; function-gate ⏳ (requires Kit RPC to confirm get_joint_velocities reports correctly-scaled max velocity at sep=1.5/0.7/0.4/0.25 m within 10% tolerance vs expected_scales)
